# Glossary

**Allergen** — A food substance that may trigger an allergic reaction. NutriFlow supports 24 allergen categories in dietary preferences.

**Cart** — A grocery shopping list of Kroger products with real prices and quantities. Can be auto-generated from a meal plan.

**Diet Type** — Your primary dietary pattern (e.g., Omnivore, Vegetarian, Keto). Set in Dietary Preferences.

**Household** — A shared group (Premium feature) where family members or roommates collaborate on meal plans, pantry, and cart.

**Insights** — The analytics dashboard showing weekly nutrition trends, streaks, goal progress, and spending data.

**Intake Log** — A daily record of foods consumed, used to track calories and macros against your goals.

**Kroger Integration** — NutriFlow connects to Kroger's API for real product names, prices, images, and store locations.

**Macro / Macronutrient** — The three major nutrients: Protein, Carbohydrates (Carbs), and Fat. Tracked in grams.

**Meal Plan** — A weekly calendar of scheduled meals across four daily slots: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snack.

**Meal Slot** — One of the four time-of-day categories in a meal plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, or Snack.

**Nutrition Goals** — Your daily targets for calories (kcal), protein (g), carbs (g), and fat (g). Set in the Profile screen.

**Pantry** — An inventory of ingredients you have at home, organized by storage type (Fridge, Frozen, Dry) with expiration tracking.

**Premium** — The paid account tier that unlocks Household collaboration, shared meal plans, receipt uploads, and advanced features.

**Recipe** — A structured set of cooking instructions with ingredients, quantities, cook time, difficulty, and nutritional information.

**Spoonacular** — A third-party recipe API that provides a large catalog of discoverable recipes within NutriFlow.

**Streak** — The number of consecutive days you have logged at least one food item. Displayed on the Home Dashboard and Insights.
